c001: What's the difference between grid trading and CLMM liquidity provision in Condor? When would you choose one over the other?
c002: My BTC/USDT grid executor has been running for 4 hours with no fills. The market has been trending upward steadily. What should I investigate and what would you recommend?
c003: My executor was stopped automatically because max drawdown was exceeded at 12%. The limit was set to 10%. Should I restart it immediately, and if so, with what parameter adjustments?
c004: I have $5,000 to trade with. How many simultaneous executors should I run, what position sizing would you recommend, and how should I spread risk across different pairs?
c005: I want to run a trend-following strategy on ETH/USDT using Condor. What type of executor and configuration would you suggest? What indicators should the strategy monitor?
c006: Explain what happens during a strategy tick in Condor. How does the agent gather market data, make decisions, and execute trades? What is the role of the journal?
c007: I'm planning a volatile altcoin grid strategy on SOL/USDT with $2,000. What risk limits should I configure for max_drawdown_pct, max_position_size, and max_open_executors?
c008: What's the difference between consult mode and loop mode for a Condor trading agent? When should I use each, and can I switch between them mid-session?
c009: How do I interpret the P&L attribution in Condor's performance reports? What does realized vs unrealized PnL mean in the context of grid trading executors?
c010: In what market conditions does grid trading outperform? When should I switch to a trend-following or DCA approach instead? Give me a framework for deciding.
c011: I'm running a pydantic-ai agent with ollama:llama3.1:8b but it never calls any tools during strategy ticks — it just writes a journal entry and stops. What's likely wrong and how do I fix it?
c012: I want to DCA into BTC over 30 days starting with $10,000. How should I configure the executor, what intervals work best, and how do I handle a flash crash scenario mid-strategy?
c013: I want to connect my Binance account to Condor so I can start trading.
c014: I want to set up a new grid trading strategy but I'm not sure which pair to use or how to size it.
c015: My grid executor on BTC/USDT has been making losses for the past 6 hours. Can you help me figure out what's happening?
sc001: I have $1,000 USDT available on Binance. Create a grid trading executor for BTC-USDT. Use 80% of the budget ($800), set the price range to ±2% from the current price, and use 10 grid levels.
sc002: Set up a dollar-cost averaging (DCA) executor on Binance to buy ETH-USDT. Buy $50 worth every 4 hours, maximum 10 DCA orders, stop if ETH drops below $2,500.
sc003: I want to set up a grid trading strategy on the XRP Ledger. Check my portfolio first, then use 90% of available funds for an XRP grid strategy.
sc004: I have a running Hummingbot bot named 'eth-maker'. Deploy a pure market making (PMM) controller for ETH-USDT on Binance with a 0.1% bid-ask spread and $500 order amount on each side.
rb001: What's the difference between a skill and a routine in Condor, and when should I write a routine instead of just checking something manually each time?
rb002: I have a routine called 'funding-rate-alert' that's supposed to run hourly and notify me when funding exceeds 0.05%, but I haven't gotten an alert in 2 days even though funding has been elevated. Can you check what's going on?
rb003: I want to write a new routine that checks BTC/ETH 30-day price correlation daily and logs the result. Read your routine_builder skill first so you follow the right structure, then check what routines already exist before creating it.
rb004: Set up a routine that pulls my portfolio's total drawdown every morning at 9am and notifies me if it exceeds 15%.
